title: Resume OS Profile
description: The keystone data contract for a tenant. This schema holds the REUSABLE structure (field meanings + the standard ATS question key set); a profiles/<id>/profile.json instance supplies one candidate's VALUES. Replaces the personal data formerly tangled into application-profile.md and the skill docs.

profileId.description: Folder id under profiles/, e.g. "amirali". Must match activeProfile in resume-os.config.json to be selected.

fullName.description: Candidate name. Leads every generated resume/PDF filename (was previously hardcoded in build-resume-formats.mjs).

contact.description: Contact block rendered on resumes and used to autofill applications.

postalCode.description: Keep the candidate's exact formatting, e.g. "V6E 4R6" with the space.

resumeLocation.description: Exact location string required in the resume contact block, e.g. Toronto, ON.

resumeContactLines.description: Exact trimmed Markdown lines required between the resume name and headline. Protects all displayed contact text and formatting.

resumeLinks.description: Canonical non-contact resume URLs. Every emitted HTTP(S) URL must be in contact.links or this list; requiredWhenText makes a URL mandatory when its project/credential marker is present.

timezone.description: Optional per-profile timezone; overrides the root resume-os.config.json timezone for this profile's job board (was hardcoded "America/Vancouver").

atsAnswers.description: Standard screening answers. The KEYS below are the reusable question set every tenant shares; the VALUES are this candidate's answers.

variantTitleOverrides.description: Per-profile overrides of the engine's variant->title map (resume-os.config.json variantTitles).

baseResumes.description: Map of variant key -> base resume filename under this profile's base-resumes/.

baseRoutingRules.description: Ordered precedence rules the route step uses to pick a base resume from a JD. Deterministic part of routing; the latent archetype read stays in the tailoring skill.

positioningFrame.description: How this candidate is positioned across resume / LinkedIn / cover letters / interview prep. Replaces the hardcoded narrative-spine and 'How <name> Uses AI As A PM' sections previously baked into resume-os.md.

aiUsageFrame.description: The 'how this person uses AI' framing, when relevant to the role.

jobSearch.description: Discovery signals/sources for this candidate's pipeline (was in application-profile.md 'Job Search' section).

frictionNotes.description: Candidate-specific autofill gotchas (e.g. field-of-study dropdown mismatches, title parsing quirks).
